mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals rising labor income and consumer spending power, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS 3-month change falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, a month-over-month decline signals that labor market healing is reducing recession probability, which triggers a compression in equity risk premia and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 8% and declines month-over-month, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate broad-based demand and supply recovery, reinforcing earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score signal of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal that real output growth is gaining traction without immediate monetary restriction, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal durable demand strength in construction and related sectors, which spills over into broader economic activity and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index raise near-term Fed tightening expectations and compress real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice policy risk.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 8%, simultaneous job gains reflected in rising nonfarm payrolls alongside falling unemployment confirm a labor market inflection that reduces tail-risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, joint increases in PAYEMS and decreases in UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances over a 3-year window.
